campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and the current state of the treasury. They also discussed plans for improving transportation around Campbell, including options for buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasury report and cash position
Multimodal transportation improvements (buses, bikes, pedestrian access)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice concerns about a new city rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first reading process and planning to vote on the final approval at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on new ordinance
Council waived first reading to speed up approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Ordinance will be placed on consent calendar for adoption

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The council reviewed records from multiple special meetings and study sessions held throughout March.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six different March 2026 meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Multiple closed-session meetings reviewed

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to organize upcoming agenda items for two future council meetings. The council reviewed and finalized the agenda for April 21st, and began planning the April 28th meeting, deciding which items needed specific time slots and handling any required legal notices for documents.
keyTopics: Finalized April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Organized April 28, 2026 draft agenda and time assignments
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which infrastructure and public projects to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month proclamation and youth recognition
National Volunteer Week acknowledgment
Capital improvements planning and priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting minutes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing code, which deals with protections for residential renters.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 commission meeting minutes
New ordinance to amend residential tenant protections program
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a new online tool that shows where Cupertino spends its money and how budgets are decided. The Council also reminded people that if you're trying to influence city decisions on behalf of a client or organization, you may need to register as a lobbyist and report your activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool - how to access and use it
City budget workshop for community input
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code
Reporting requirements for those influencing city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026, to discuss updates to the city's Charter (its basic governing rules). Council Chair Pat Nikolai joined from Oregon rather than in person. Residents could attend and comment either in person or online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings being held to update city governing rules
Chair Pat Nikolai participating remotely from Curtin, Oregon
Remote participation options available via Zoom for residents
Public comment deadline: email cityattorney@santaclaraca.gov before 10 a.m.

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to handle routine business and review several important planning documents. Key items included updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, consideration of changing rules for how council vacancies are filled, and review of council members' requests for new projects to work on.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan update (2025-2030 Consolidated Plan)
Changes to council vacancy procedures
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting permission to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space in exchange for renovating the building to meet modern seismic safety standards and preserve its historic character.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zoning district (CD-C)
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Approval of previous board meeting minutes from March 12, 2026
